design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate maze platforms, walk full length of burger ingredients to drop them onto buns below, complete burgers while avoiding/managing enemies using limited pepper shots
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure and spatial constraint: player must carefully navigate platforms to complete burger construction while being hunted by three types of mobile enemies (Hot Dog, Pickle, Egg) that respawn, forcing constant threat management
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Chef heroics - transform oversized burger components into complete meals through precise platforming and tactical enemy avoidance; comedic power fantasy of using pepper to stun food adversaries
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces puzzle-like spatial awareness and risk-reward calculations rather than reflexive action. The pepper system is deliberately limited, forcing players to choose when to use this resource strategically. Dropping enemies for bonus points is riskier but faster than crushing them—the game rewards skilled, aggressive play but doesn't require it, allowing multiple valid playstyles.

difficulty.lives_system: Player starts with multiple lives; one life lost per enemy touch (when not stunned by pepper). Game ends when all lives exhausted.
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints within boards; death resets to start of current level. Six boards cycle with increasing complexity.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player can choose safe crushing strategy (drop ingredients on enemies passively) or risky speed strategy (drop enemies for bonus points and faster completion). Pepper usage is player-controlled. Board layouts become progressively harder but difficulty scales gradually across six repeating cycles.

progression.upgrade_system: Pepper shots (5 starting; bonus shots awarded by food pickups like ice cream, coffee, fries that appear after set ingredient drops). Extra lives awarded at variable point thresholds (varies by port).
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six boards of increasing difficulty unlock sequentially; after board 6 completes, cycle repeats with same boards but escalated enemy behavior and ingredient arrangements.
replayability_hooks: Score chasing via risky enemy-dropping tactics
Multiple valid strategic approaches (safe vs. aggressive play)
Skill-based optimization of burger completion routes
Bonus food pickup hunting for pepper management

world_building_techniques: Character archetypes (Chef Peter Pepper protagonist vs. anthropomorphic food enemies as antagonists)
Implicit narrative through visual design: oversized ingredients, chef-vs-food conflict framed as comedic
Maze environments establish settings without explicit exposition

faction_indicators: Chef/human (Peter Pepper) vs. Food entities (anthropomorphic Hot Dog, Pickle, Egg)
danger_signals: Enemy food items persistently chase player through mazes
Crushing or dropping enemies produces score rewards but requires risky positioning
Limited pepper supply signals resource scarcity and tactical choice necessity
environmental_storytelling: Maze platforms with burger ingredients arranged vertically communicate burger assembly as core mechanic
Ingredient variety (bun, patty, tomato, lettuce, cheese) establishes mundane food-preparation context
Edges and respawn points for enemies create implicit 'safe zones' vs. active threat zones

sources_used: Wikipedia: BurgerTime (gameplay section, ports section, platform/release information)
The Cutting Room Floor: BurgerTime (NES, Famicom Disk System) (metadata)
